Facts.text: Battle of Siffin occurs during the first Muslim civil war between Ali ibn Abi Talib and Muawiyah I beside the Euphrates River
Battle of Pliska: Bulgarians under Khan Krum beat a Byzantine army, killing the Emperor Nicephorus, whose skull Krum has made into a drinking cup
Rout of an alliance of Christian troops from Navarre and Léon against the Muslims at Pamplona
Inquisition forms in Rome under Pope Clement IV
Henry VII is recognized King of the Romans by Pope Clement V
Spanish conquistador Alonso de Ojeda is the first European to sight Curacao Island
Francisco Pizarroappointed Governor of Peru
Spanish troops conquer Aalst
English Admiral John Hawkins knighted for his actions against the Armada
English mathematician Thomas Harriot is the first person to draw a map of the Moon by looking through a telescope
French troops invade papal territory Comtat Venaissin
England and Netherlands sign Treaty of Alliance at the Hague, Netherlands, formalizing co-operative defense against France
Battle at Hastenbeck: French army beats Duke of Cumberland
11,000 British troops drive a token French garrison of 400 out of Fort Ticonderoga, New York
Austrian troops occupy Fort Glatz during Third Silesian War
US Congress passes the Funding Act of 1790 making the federal government responsible for debts incurred by the states
The Surrey Iron Railway, arguably the world's first public railway, opens in south London
Naples and Calabria are struck by an earthquake, killing about 26,000 people
Riots in Vilnius, Lithuania, cause the death of many Jews
HMS Beagle anchors in Montevideo
First sugarcane plantation starts in Hawaii
Moses Gerrish Farmer builds first miniature train for children to ride
The Liberian Declaration of Independence is signed making Liberia a sovereign nation, independent from the American Colonization Society
Baron Lionel de Rothschild becomes the first Jewish person to be elected to the British Parliament
US Civil War: Union Brigadier General Edward McCook leads unsuccessful cavalry raids at Lovejoy's Station, Georgia
US Civil War: Union General George Stoneman leads unsuccessful raid in Macon, Georgia
Patrick Francis Healy is the first African American awarded a PhD from the University of Leuven, Belgium
The capital of New Zealand moves from Auckland to Wellington
The Royal Canoe Club, founded by John MacGregor, opens in England

In California, poet and American West outlaw calling himself "Black Bart" makes his last clean getaway when he steals a safe box from a Wells Fargo stagecoach. The empty box is found later with a taunting poem inside.
French marines occupy the Tunisian harbor city of Sfax
First Esperanto book is published
France annexes Tahiti
Henry James's "American" premieres in London
Vitascope Hall, the first permanent for-profit movie theater, opens in New Orleans
37.5 cm of rainfall is recorded at Jewell, Maryland (state record)
Australia defeats England by three runs at Old Trafford

Horatio Nelson Jackson, Sewall K. Crocker, and a bulldog named Bud complete the first automobile trip across the United States, traveling from San Francisco to New York in a two-cylinder Winton in 63 days, 12 hours, and 30 minutes
US Attorney General Charles Joseph Bonaparte issues an order to immediately staff the Office of the Chief Examiner (later renamed the Federal Bureau of Investigation)

Britain attempts to organize a conference among major European powers to resolve the dispute between Austria-Hungary and Serbia; France, Italy, and eventually Russia agree to participate, but Germany refuses

Irish Volunteers unload a shipment of 1,500 rifles and 45,000 rounds of ammunition from Germany aboard Erskine Childers' yacht, the Asgard; British troops fire on a jeering crowd on Bachelors Walk, Dublin, killing three citizens
The US protests the "Blacklist" issued by the British, forbidding trade with some 30 US firms
Race riot in Philadelphia kills three white people and one black person
National Bar Association incorporates
Philippines government asks USA for a plebiscite on independence
Gene Tunney scores an 11-round TKO win over Tom Heeney at Yankee Stadium, NYC, in only his second and final defense of his world heavyweight boxing title
New York Yankees score 11 runs in 12th inning, defeating Tigers 12-1 in Detroit
International communist conference members in Prague arrange create International Brigades to help the Republican Government in the Spanish Civil War
End of the Battle of Brunete in the Spanish Civil War
First radio broadcast of "Young Widder Brown" on NBC
Yankee catcher Bill Dickey hits three consecutive home runs
First group of the Dutch Volunteer Legion departs from Den Haag Central train station in The Hague for the Eastern Front to fight on the side of Germany
US embargo on oil export to Japan
Roman Catholic churches protest as Dutch bishops stand against the spread of Judaism
120°F (49°C) in Tishmoningo, Oklahoma (state record)
Otto Skorzeny's commando group arrives in Rome
Japanese suicide attack on US lines in Guam
Soviet troops arrive in Weichsel
The first German V-2 rocket hits Great Britain (nicknamed "gasometer")
US offensive at St-Lô/2nd Armored Division occupies St. Gilles
Japanese government disregards US ultimatum
US cruiser Indianapolis reaches Tinian with atomic bomb
Winston Churchillresigns as Britain's Prime Minister after election defeat
Aloha Airlines begins service from Honolulu International Airport
First Black host of a network show: Bob Howard Show on CBS
President Harry Truman issues Executive Order No. 9981 to desegregate the US armed forces, directing "equality of treatment and opportunity" in the military
WCPO TV channel 9 in Cincinnati, OH (CBS) begins broadcasting
Dodgers' Jim Russell is the first to switch-hit home runs twice in a game
KNIL (Royal Dutch East Indies Army) unites
Netherlands ends state of war with Germany
King Farouk I of Egypt abdicates (Black Saturday)
Arizona Governor John Howard Pyle orders an anti-polygamy law enforcement crackdown on residents of Short Creek, Arizona, which becomes known as the Short Creek Raid
Cuban pirate radio station's first transmission in Santiago de Cuba
WCET TV channel 48 in Cincinnati, OH (PBS) begins broadcasting
Last day as Test cricket umpire for Frank Chester
Ted Allen throws a record 72 consecutive horseshoe ringers
Carlos Castillo Armas, dictator and president of Guatemala, is assassinated by a palace guard with leftist sympathies
Mickey Mantlehits career home run number 200
Army launches fourth successful US satellite, Explorer IV
Italian government of Fanfani forms
Maria Oeljanov, first airship with nuclear missiles, arrives in Cuba
Skopje in Yugoslavia is destroyed by an earthquake, killing 1,000+
The Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development votes to admit Japan
US Syncom 2, the first geosynchronous communications satellite, launches
Teamsters President and US union leader Jimmy Hoffa convicted of fraud and conspiracy
Train from Povoa de Varzin, Portugal, derails near Oporto, killing 94
Republic of Maldives gains independence from Britain (National Day)
Lord Gardiner issues the Practice Statement in the House of Lords stating that the House is not bound to follow its own previous precedent
WRLH TV channel 31 in Lebanon, NH (NBC) begins broadcasting
Minnesota Twins defeat the Yankees 3-2 in 18 innings
Sharon Sites Adams, 39, becomes the first woman to solo sail the Pacific
Apollo 15 launches (Scott and Irwin) for the fourth manned landing on the Moon
Rockwell receives NASA contract to construct Space Shuttle
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
USSR's Soyuz fails to dock with Salyut 3
Soviet Soyuz 18B returns to Earth
The Committee of Ten, formed by prominent Soweto residents, issues a program for the election of a new community board to have total autonomy in Soweto, South Africa
USSR performs underground nuclear test
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Johnny Benchhits his 300th career home run
Estimated 109 cm (43 inches) of rain falls in Alvin, Texas (national record)
Pioneer 11 transmits images of Saturn and its rings
Two climbers fall 550 m down cliff near Angel Falls, Venezuela
Canada's Anik D1 Comsat is launched by a US Delta rocket
Challenger moves to the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida for mating for STS-8
Jarmila Kratochvilova of Czech sets 800 m woman's record (1:53.28)
Light flashes are seen on Jupiter's moon Io
US threatens action to preserve navigation in Persian Gulf
Lebanese kidnappers release Rev Lawrence Martin Jenco
Pitcher Jim "Catfish" Hunter,  outfielder Billy Williams, and third baseman Ray Dandridge are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York
General Hospital records its 7,000th episode
US defeats Soviet Union 17-0 in baseball at Goodwill Games
Actor Paul Reubens (Pee-wee Herman) is arrested for exposing himself at an adult movie theater in Sarasota, Florida
CFL assumes ownership of Ottawa Rough Riders
Expos' Mark Gardner no-hits Dodgers for 9 innings but loses in 10th
Britain honors her dead in the Falklands War
Boeing 737-500 crashes in South Korea, killing 66
The first of four photos of Mars is taken by the Mars Observer just under a month before the spacecraft fails in flight
Cambodia's Khmer Rouge surprise attack on train kills 13
Turkish Air Force bombs Kurds in Iraq, killing 70
Fighting ceases between India and Pakistan in the Kargil War, Kashmir, after two months of fighting
Mumbai, India receives 99.5 cm (39.17 inches) of rain within 24 hours, bringing the city to a halt for over 2 days
Samir Geagea, the Lebanese Forces (LF) leader, is released after spending 11 years in solitary confinement; his release comes after Syria ends its occupation of Lebanon
Space Shuttle program: STS-114 Mission - Launch of Discovery, NASA's first scheduled flight mission after the Columbia disaster in 2003
At least 200 people are killed in a day of violence in Syria
Insurgent attack kills 19 people and destroys a helicopter in Baqubah, Iraq
North Korea is hit by Tropical Storm Khanun, killing 88 people and leaving 60,000 people homeless
57 people are killed in a market bombing in Parachinar, Pakistan
Brian Ortega's victory over Mike De La Torre is overturned to "No Contest" after testing positive for drostanolone
Taghrooda wins the King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes
The Chinese government suspends a Shanghai meat dealer and makes arrests after the company sold out-of-date meat to fast food chains, including McDonald's and Kentucky Fried Chicken
While Israel rejects a long-term ceasefire that does not include destroying the Hamas tunnels, they agree to a 12-hour ceasefire; the Palestinian death toll in the Gaza Strip now exceeds 1,000
Great Britain announces it will ban gasoline and diesel cars by 2040
Three live king cobras are found inside potato chip cans by customs officials in Los Angeles
Authorities in Stung Treng province, Cambodia, evacuate 25,000 below collapsed Laos dam as waters rise
Facebook has the single worst day of any public company on the stock market, losing 19% or $119 billion market value
Over 700 immigrant children still separated from their parents in the US as the court-imposed deadline to reunite them passes
Australian cricket captain Meg Lanning records a T20 International world record individual score of 133 off 63 balls in a 93-run win against England at Chelmsford; Southern Stars clinch Women's Ashes
Ship carrying migrants wrecks off the coast of Al-Khums, Libya, killing at least 57 and bringing the death toll in the central Mediterranean to 987 for 2021
Tunisian government is in crisis after President Kais Saied sacks Prime Minister Hichem Mechichi and suspends parliament with the help of the army due to its handling of COVID-19
Weightlifter Hidilyn Diaz becomes the first athlete from the Philippines to win gold at the Olympics in the 55 kg class at the Tokyo Games
Russia announces it will leave the International Space Station at the end of 2024
Two new studies published in "Science" confirm COVID-19 most likely began in the Huanan Seafood Market in Wuhan, China
Cambodia's authoritarian Prime Minister Hun Sen, in power since 1985, announces he will resign and pass his office to his son
Coup in Niger as presidential guards seize President Mohamed Bazoum and ministerial buildings in Niamey
Former military intelligence officer David Grusch testifies to a US House committee that the US government is being kept in the dark about unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs or UFOs)
World's largest office building, Surat Diamond Bourse, at 660,000 square meters (bigger than the Pentagon), is unveiled in Surat, India, by architectural studio Morphogenesis
XXXIII Summer Olympic Games officially open along the River Seine and at Jardins du Trocadéro, in front of the Eiffel Tower, Paris
Almost 140,000 civilians in Thailand and an estimated 38,000 in Cambodia flee their homes as fighting between the two countries intensifies
Australian Defense Secretary Richard Marles and British Defense Secretary John Healey sign a 50-year bilateral Nuclear-Powered Submarine Partnership and Collaboration Treaty in Geelong, Australia
Francisco Pizarroreceives a royal charter for the west coast of South America
James Vdeclared fit to govern by the Scottish Parliament at age 12
Dutch Act of Abjuration declared - seven Dutch provinces declare their independence from Spanish KingPhilip II

Dutch painterRembrandt van Rijndeclares he is insolvent after living beyond his means and spending too much on artwork and rare items. Leads to the most experimental and exuberantly creative period of his career.
Giacomo Casanovais arrested in Venice for affront to religion and common decency and imprisoned in the Doge's Palace
British battle fleet under generalJames Wolfeconquers Louisbourg
US Continental Congress creates United States Post Office (U.S.P.O.) in Philadelphia underBenjamin Franklin
Secret meeting ofSimón Bolívarand Jose de San Martin
Battle of Salineville: Confederate cavalry leaderJohn Hunt Morganand 364 troops surrender to Union forces after a 17-day unauthorized raid through Indiana and Ohio
Richard Wagner's opera "Parsifal" premieres in Bayreuth, Germany
First Lord of the Admiralty (British Minister of Navy)Winston Churchillorders British fleet to remain in state of readiness as threat of war in Europe grows
J. Edgar Hoovergets job in US Department of Justice
Joe DiMaggioends 61-game hitting streak in Pacific Coast League
After a Labour landslide in the general election,Clement Attleebecomes the British Prime Minister
USPresident Trumansigns the National Security Act of 1947, establishing the Department of Defense, the CIA, the National Security Council, and the Joint Chiefs of Staff
"Babe RuthStory" premieres; Babe Ruth makes his final public appearance
Leo Durocherreturns to Ebbets Field as manager of the New York Giants
Baseball playerMickey Mantlehits his first grand slam for the New York Yankees
Fidel Castroleads a failed attack on the Moncada Barracks, intended to spark a revolution in Cuba
Egyptian PresidentGamal Abdel Nasserannounces a plan to nationalize the Suez Canal, initiating the Suez Crisis
Chicago White Sox'sLarry Dobyplays his final MLB game and retires in 1962 after playing for the Chunichi Dragons in Japan
Milwaukee BraveWarren Spahnsets home run record of 31 by a pitcher

Reds catcherJohnny Bench, playing for the day in the outfield, goes 4-for-5 with seven RBIs, including three consecutive home runs off St. Louis Cardinals'Steve Carltonin a 12-5 win at Riverfront Stadium, Cincinnati
PriscillaandElvis Presleyfile for legal separation
Peter Shaffer's musical "Equus" premieres in London
New York MayorEd Kochis given the Heimlich maneuver in a Chinese restaurant
Indian film starAmitabh Bachchanis seriously injured filming a fight scene for the movie "Coolie"
Bowie Kuhnannounces pitcherVida Blueis suspended for the rest of 1984 due to cocaine conviction
Montreal Expos infielderPete Roseties Ty Cobb with hit #3,502
Mike Schmidtsets an NL record by appearing in 2,155 games at third base as the Phillies and NY Mets end the game at 2:13 am
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushsigns Americans With Disabilities Act
Nolan Ryanstrikes out his 100th batter for the 23rd consecutive season
Brian Ortegamakes his UFC debut against Mike De La Torre on UFC on Fox 12
US PresidentDonald Trumpannounces a policy to ban transgender people from the military, overturningObama-era changes
Observation of a black hole by the Very Large Telescope in Chile provesAlbert Einstein's prediction of "gravitational redshift," published in "Astronomy & Astrophysics"
SirPaul McCartneyperforms a "secret" gig at the Cavern Club in Liverpool, where the Beatles began
Body of civil rights activistJohn Lewiscrosses the Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma for the last time, beaten there 55 years ago, with a military honor guard as part of a remembrance ceremony
Italian football giants Juventus win their ninth straight Serie A title with goals fromCristiano Ronaldoand Federico Bernardeschi in a 2-0 win over Sampdoria
Dominant British breaststroke swimmerAdam Peatysuccessfully defends his 100 m title at the Tokyo Olympics, defeating Arno Kamminga of the Netherlands and Italian Nicolò Martinenghi
ActorKevin Spaceyis cleared of sex assault charges at a court in London
Marvel film "Deadpool and Wolverine," starringRyan ReynoldsandHugh Jackman, opens with the highest earnings of the year: $233.3 million in the US and $438 million globally
Opening ceremony for the XXXIII Olympiad in Paris is held outside for the first time along the River Seine, with 85 boats carrying the athletes and performances byLady GagaandCeline Dion
Beyoncéends the Cowboy Carter Tour with a Destiny's Child reunion, the first time Beyoncé, Michelle Williams, and Kelly Rowland have performed together in seven years
Francisco Pizarroorders the death of the last Sapa Inca Emperor, Atahualpa
New York becomes 11th state to ratify US constitution

PhysicistRaemer Schreiberand Lieutenant Colonel Peer de Silva depart Kirtland Army Air Field to transport the plutonium core for the Fat Man bomb (bombing of Nagasaki) to the island of Tinian, where the bomb is assembled
US, Britain, and China demand the unconditional surrender of Japan during World War II in the Declaration of Potsdam
